_note: F5 batch TAKE-2 (buried-but-present). Same 10 fixed diseases as take-1, but the disease is now NAMED EXACTLY ONCE in each model_desc, buried in the FINAL clause (never the opening sentence, never repeated). use_case/population/setting never restate the disease. This recreates the heart-failure demo condition (disease present-but-buried) that wide-net recovers and frozen misses. Oracles are canonical NLM MeSH preferred terms, used ONLY for scoring (never passed to the engine). intervention_type='device' for all 10 (imaging/EHR/signal SaMD, no drugs). Source: Claude Science take-2, 2026-07-11.

cases.model_desc: A dual-branch architecture pairs a 3D convolutional backbone (an inflated ResNet-50 operating on 64×64×64 voxel patches resampled to 1 mm isotropic spacing) with a hand-crafted radiomics branch (wavelet-decomposed first-order statistics, gray-level co-occurrence and run-length texture descriptors extracted from the segmented nodule volume). Nodule candidates are localized by an upstream U-Net detector, then each candidate volume is passed through the CNN branch; feature maps are aggregated with a gated multi-instance attention pooling layer so that scan-level prediction is driven by the most suspicious nodule. The radiomics vector is concatenated with the pooled deep embedding and fed to a two-layer fully connected head with dropout, emitting a calibrated malignancy probability plus an auxiliary regression output for doubling-time estimation. The model is trained to stratify indeterminate pulmonary nodules by likelihood of progression to non-small cell lung cancer.
cases.use_case: Assigns each screen-detected pulmonary nodule a calibrated malignancy-risk score and estimated growth rate to prioritize biopsy versus surveillance.
cases.population: Adults aged 50–80 with a ≥20 pack-year smoking history and indeterminate pulmonary nodules found on low-dose screening CT.
cases.setting: Outpatient lung cancer screening programs at tertiary imaging centers.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...likelihood of progression to non-small cell lung cancer.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: A 3D encoder–decoder segmentation network (nnU-Net configuration) consumes co-registered non-contrast CT and CT-perfusion parameter maps (cerebral blood flow, cerebral blood volume, Tmax, mean transit time) as multi-channel input, with a symmetry-aware feature branch that subtracts contralateral hemisphere activations to accentuate asymmetric hypoperfusion. Skip connections carry high-resolution edge features to the decoder, which outputs voxelwise probabilities for infarct core and salvageable penumbra; a downstream regression head integrates the segmented volumes to produce a mismatch ratio. Post-processing applies connected-component filtering and an ASPECTS-region atlas overlay to yield per-territory scores. The pipeline is designed to quantify core–penumbra mismatch for triage of acute ischemic stroke.
cases.use_case: Automatically segments irreversibly injured tissue versus at-risk tissue on emergency perfusion imaging and computes a mismatch ratio to guide thrombectomy eligibility.
cases.population: Adults presenting within 24 hours of symptom onset with suspected large-vessel occlusion.
cases.setting: Emergency department and neuro-interventional suite of a comprehensive stroke center.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...for triage of acute ischemic stroke.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: A hybrid tabular–imaging model fuses a gradient-boosted decision tree ensemble over spirometry-derived features (post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC ratio, FEV1 percent predicted, forced expiratory flow slopes) and longitudinal symptom scores with a 2.5D convolutional network that quantifies low-attenuation-area emphysema percentage and airway wall thickness from inspiratory/expiratory chest CT. Deep imaging embeddings are distilled into a compact feature vector via global average pooling and concatenated with the tabular features; a stacked meta-learner (logistic regression over out-of-fold predictions) produces the final output. A survival head modeled as a discrete-time hazard predicts time-to-next-exacerbation. The system estimates one-year exacerbation risk in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.
cases.use_case: Predicts twelve-month risk of acute exacerbation from lung-function testing, symptom diaries, and quantitative CT airway/emphysema metrics.
cases.population: Adults ≥40 with airflow obstruction confirmed on spirometry and a prior exacerbation history.
cases.setting: Outpatient pulmonology clinics and integrated care management programs.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...in patients with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: A sequence model processes irregularly sampled electronic-health-record time series — serial serum creatinine, hourly urine-output rates, vital signs, nephrotoxic medication administration events, and fluid balance — using a time-aware LSTM whose forget gate is modulated by inter-observation time gaps, augmented by a masking-and-decay embedding for missing values. A multi-head self-attention layer over the hidden-state sequence highlights the observation windows most predictive of imminent deterioration, and a sigmoid head emits a rolling 48-hour risk score refreshed at each new lab result. Class imbalance is handled with focal loss, and outputs are calibrated by isotonic regression on a held-out temporal split. The model issues early warnings for impending acute kidney injury.
cases.use_case: Continuously computes a rolling 48-hour probability of renal deterioration from streaming laboratory, vital-sign, and medication data.
cases.population: Adult inpatients without pre-existing end-stage renal disease admitted for medical or surgical care.
cases.setting: Acute-care hospital wards and intensive care units with real-time EHR integration.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...for impending acute kidney injury.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: A convolutional classifier built on a DenseNet-121 backbone pretrained on large chest-radiograph corpora ingests frontal chest X-rays resampled to 512×512, with lung-field segmentation from a preprocessing U-Net used to mask non-thoracic pixels and normalize the field of view. The network is fine-tuned with class-balanced sampling; Grad-CAM heatmaps localize cavitation, upper-lobe infiltrates, and miliary patterns to provide radiologist-facing explanations. The final fully connected layer outputs an abnormality probability, and an ensemble of five models trained under different augmentation seeds is averaged to reduce variance. The classifier is optimized to flag radiographic findings suggestive of pulmonary tuberculosis.
cases.use_case: Screens frontal chest radiographs for abnormalities suggestive of active infection and returns a probability score with a spatial heatmap for confirmatory review.
cases.population: Adults and adolescents undergoing symptomatic or contact-based screening in high-incidence regions.
cases.setting: Community screening campaigns and primary-care clinics with portable digital radiography.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...findings suggestive of pulmonary tuberculosis.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: An EfficientNet-B4 backbone processes macula-centered color fundus photographs at 456×456 resolution after contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalization and circular field-of-view cropping, trained with an ordinal regression objective that respects the monotonic severity scale rather than treating grades as independent classes. Test-time augmentation averages predictions over flips and rotations, and an attention-gated feature map surfaces microaneurysms, hemorrhages, and hard exudates as lesion-level evidence. A quality-assessment sub-network gates ungradable images out of the pipeline before scoring. The system outputs a five-level severity grade to identify referable diabetic retinopathy.
cases.use_case: Grades retinal photographs on a five-point severity scale and flags cases meeting the threshold for specialist referral.
cases.population: Adults with diabetes mellitus enrolled in routine annual eye-screening.
cases.setting: Primary-care and community optometry screening with tele-ophthalmology review.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...to identify referable diabetic retinopathy.' (once, standard name). NOTE: 'diabetes mellitus' also appears in population.

cases.model_desc: A convolutional network (ViT-hybrid: a CNN stem feeding a vision-transformer encoder) ingests dermoscopic images with paired patient metadata (anatomic site, age, sex) embedded and injected as additional tokens, enabling cross-attention between lesion morphology and clinical context. Training uses a balanced sampler across skin-tone and lesion-type strata, with self-supervised contrastive pretraining on unlabeled dermoscopy to improve representation of rare patterns; the classification head emits a malignancy probability alongside the seven-point checklist attribute predictions (atypical network, blue-white veil, irregular streaks) as auxiliary outputs. Monte Carlo dropout provides an uncertainty estimate that abstains on low-confidence cases. The model is trained to distinguish benign pigmented lesions from melanoma.
cases.use_case: Classifies dermoscopic images of pigmented skin lesions as benign or malignant and reports interpretable morphologic attributes with an uncertainty flag.
cases.population: Adults presenting with suspicious or changing pigmented skin lesions.
cases.setting: Dermatology outpatient clinics and teledermatology triage services.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...benign pigmented lesions from melanoma.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: A multimodal model combines a convolutional endoscopic-image branch (ResNet-34 scoring mucosal ulceration, stenosis, and inflammatory extent frame by frame from ileocolonoscopy video) with a tabular branch over laboratory markers (fecal calprotectin, C-reactive protein, hemoglobin, albumin) and a transformer encoder over free-text pathology and clinical notes. Frame-level endoscopic scores are aggregated by a recurrent pooling layer into a segment-level activity index, then late-fused with the laboratory and text embeddings through a gated attention block; the output head predicts probability of relapse within twelve months and an auxiliary endoscopic-severity index. The pipeline is intended to forecast disease flares in patients with Crohn's disease.
cases.use_case: Integrates endoscopic video, inflammatory biomarkers, and clinical text to predict twelve-month relapse probability and quantify mucosal inflammatory activity.
cases.population: Adults with established inflammatory bowel disease in clinical remission or mild activity.
cases.setting: Gastroenterology specialty clinics and inflammatory bowel disease centers.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...in patients with Crohn's disease.' (once, standard name). NOTE: parent term 'inflammatory bowel disease' appears in population/setting.

cases.model_desc: A gradient-boosted tree ensemble (XGBoost) operates on structured primary-care features — body-mass index, waist circumference, fasting glucose, HbA1c, blood pressure, lipid panel, family history, and age — with engineered interaction terms and spline-transformed continuous variables to capture nonlinear metabolic thresholds. SHAP values provide per-patient additive feature attributions for clinician interpretability, and monotonic constraints enforce clinically expected directional relationships (e.g., risk non-decreasing in HbA1c). Probabilities are recalibrated with Platt scaling on a temporally held-out cohort. The model estimates five-year risk of incident type 2 diabetes mellitus.
cases.use_case: Estimates five-year probability of incident disease from routine metabolic labs and anthropometric data, with per-patient feature attributions.
cases.population: Adults ≥35 without a current diagnosis, attending routine health checks.
cases.setting: Primary-care practices and population health screening programs.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...risk of incident type 2 diabetes mellitus.' (once, standard name)

cases.model_desc: A transformer-based natural-language-processing model (a clinically fine-tuned BERT encoder) processes free-text clinical-encounter notes and patient-reported questionnaire responses, with a hierarchical attention structure that pools token-level representations to sentence embeddings and then to a document embedding. A parallel tabular branch encodes structured features (prior symptom-scale scores, sleep and psychomotor items, medication history) and is fused with the text embedding via concatenation before a softmax head predicts symptom-severity trajectory class. Adversarial debiasing is applied on demographic attributes to reduce disparate error rates, and attention weights surface the phrases driving each prediction. The system stratifies severity trajectory in patients with major depressive disorder.
cases.use_case: Predicts symptom-severity trajectory class from clinical notes, questionnaire text, and structured symptom history to guide treatment intensification.
cases.population: Adults undergoing evaluation or treatment for a mood disorder.
cases.setting: Outpatient behavioral-health and integrated primary-care/collaborative-care settings.
cases._disease_in_text: model_desc final clause: '...in patients with major depressive disorder.' (once, standard name)
